new_files.purpose: Generic virtual scrolling list widget for efficient rendering of large datasets
Central export point for core widgets to provide consistent import experience
Comprehensive documentation with 6 usage examples, performance guidelines, and migration guide
Six example implementations demonstrating various VirtualListView features

modified_files.details: Added completion record for phase-2-subtask-2 with comprehensive feature list and verification
Updated subtask status from 'pending' to 'completed' and added detailed completion notes

architecture.widget_placement: Follows existing pattern of placing reusable widgets in lib/core/widgets/
architecture.state_management: Uses StatelessWidget with const constructors following codebase conventions
architecture.export_strategy: Uses barrel export pattern (widgets.dart) for consistent imports
architecture.documentation: Comprehensive README with examples, performance considerations, and migration guides

implementation_patterns.virtual_scrolling: Built on Flutter's ListView.builder (no external dependencies)
implementation_patterns.type_safety: Generic type parameter T for type-safe item rendering
implementation_patterns.convenience_api: Extension methods for common scenarios (vertical, horizontal constructors)
implementation_patterns.state_handling: Built-in loading, error, and empty state widgets
implementation_patterns.flexibility: Optional parameters for headers, footers, separators, fixed extent

performance_considerations: itemExtent should be used when items have fixed height for better performance
Separators double widget count - keep them simple
Avoid complex widgets in itemBuilder for smooth scrolling
Use const constructors for item widgets when possible
usage_guidelines: VirtualListView vs ListView.builder decision tree based on list size
Pagination recommendation for extremely large lists (1000+ items)
Migration guide provided for easy transition from ListView.builder

success_factors: Comprehensive feature set covering all common list use cases
No external dependencies - uses Flutter's built-in virtual scrolling
Type-safe implementation with generic type parameter
Consistent API following existing codebase patterns
Ready for immediate use in subsequent phases
achievements: Implemented efficient virtual scrolling for 500+ items
Updated project tracking and planning documents

immediate_next_steps: Begin phase-2-subtask-3: Implement virtualized trip items list
Integrate VirtualListView into trip items/activities screens
Add performance metrics to virtual lists (phase-2-subtask-5)
best_practices: Use VirtualListView for all lists with 50+ items
Monitor memory usage with large datasets
